op.name: linalg.matrix_power
op.notes: Matrix power. Cost: $(\lfloor\log_2 k\rfloor + \text{popcount}(k) - 1) \cdot n^3$ (exponentiation by squaring).
op.summary: Raise a square matrix to the (integer) power `n`.

blocks.text: Raise a square matrix to the (integer) power `n`.

For positive integers `n`, the power is computed by repeated matrix
squarings and matrix multiplications. If ``n == 0``, the identity matrix
of the same shape as M is returned. If ``n < 0``, the inverse
is computed and then raised to the ``abs(n)``.

blocks.name: note:: Stacks of object matrices are not currently supported.
blocks.raw: .. note:: Stacks of object matrices are not currently supported.

desc_blocks.text:
The exponent can be any integer or long integer, positive,
negative, or zero.

items.name: a**n
items.type: (..., M, M) ndarray or matrix object

desc_blocks.text:
The return value is the same shape and type as `M`;
if the exponent is positive or zero then the type of the
elements is the same as those of `M`. If the exponent is
negative the elements are floating-point.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> from numpy.linalg import matrix_power
>>> i = np.array([[0, 1], [-1, 0]]) # matrix equiv. of the imaginary unit
>>> matrix_power(i, 3) # should = -i
array([[ 0, -1],
       [ 1,  0]])
>>> matrix_power(i, 0)
array([[1, 0],
       [0, 1]])
>>> matrix_power(i, -3) # should = 1/(-i) = i, but w/ f.p. elements
array([[ 0.,  1.],
       [-1.,  0.]])
